description: A land-use planner needs to identify sites suitable for development by combining satellite vegetation data with terrain elevation. Using 'landsat_multispectral.tif' (SWIR1=B1, NIR=B2, Red=B3, Green=B4) and 'srtm_dem.tif' (single band: elevation) from ~/snap_data/, collocate both datasets into a single product with the Landsat image as the master. Create a virtual band named 'NDVI' computing the Normalized Difference Vegetation Index from NIR and Red bands, and a virtual band named 'suitability_class' that classifies each pixel: value 3 (Suitable) where NDVI > 0.3 and elevation < 500, value 2 (Marginal) where NDVI > 0.15 and elevation < 800, and value 1 (Unsuitable) for all remaining pixels. Create a 200x200 pixel spatial subset (pixel coordinates 100 to 299 in both X and Y). Save the full collocated product as BEAM-DIMAP to ~/snap_projects/suitability_assessment.dim and export the spatial subset as GeoTIFF to ~/snap_exports/suitability_subset.tif.
